tried_strategies.goal: Dense embedding for external real codebase

tried_strategies.notes: Flask IMPL +4.6%p, FastAPI SEMA +5.5%p, FastAPI overall +2.4%p. Large-repo IMPLICIT fallback tried and REVERTED (made things worse).
Flask EXPL +9.5%p, FastAPI EXPL +4.6%p, mean external +2.2%p. IMPL slightly degraded from IDF shifts.

Flask SEMA +14.3%p, Requests SEMA +11.4%p, Flask TEMP +20%p, Requests TEMP +10%p. External mean 0.5649→0.6033 (+3.8%p). Crossed 0.600 target. FastAPI ceiling ~0.41 due to benchmark data quality issues (duplicate queries, 928-file corpus).

Flask R@5 0.6003→0.6609 (+6.1%p), Requests 0.6928→0.7526 (+6.0%p), FastAPI unchanged (structural ceiling). Mean external 0.5661→0.6067. Temporal noise reduction and semantic improvements worked on medium repos (Flask 83, Requests 36 files) but not large (FastAPI 1120).

dead_ends.reason: Dense embedding degrades Code→Code retrieval (external R@5: 0.547→0.441). Structural signals (symbol/import graph) dominate for developer workflow queries.

dead_ends.goal: IMPLICIT_CONTEXT large-repo fallback to concept_retrieve (>200 files)
dead_ends.reason: FastAPI IMPL 0.051→0.025 (worse). concept_ref for IMPLICIT queries not compatible with concept_retrieve BM25 approach

known_facts: trigger_classifier.py: snake_case SYMBOL_PATTERN, EXPLICIT F1=1.0, accuracy=100%
paper: section 4.8=External (Table 3), 4.9=COIR (Table 4) — correct sequential order
paper: Table 5 = Ablation Study (labeled)
paper: Reference [6] Memori = arXiv:2603.19935 (2026)
paper: Reference [12] jCodeMunch = GitHub software tool (jgravelle/jcodemunch-mcp)
paper: Contribution 1 = deterministic, zero-latency, model-agnostic (vs learned classifiers)
paper: Limitations includes ground-truth circularity for IMPLICIT_CONTEXT
paper: Conclusion includes deployment guidance (CTX/Hybrid/BM25 workload conditions)
paper: Future Work has quantitative targets (pytest, CCS 0.180→0.72, n≥500)
paper: COIR hybrid improvement = '2.5× over CTX alone' (NOT 150% — reserved for IMPLICIT_CONTEXT)
paper: All three '150%' uses are exclusively IMPLICIT_CONTEXT (lines 35, 447, 473)
paper: OneViral TES ratio = 3.6x over BM25 (0.232/0.065=3.57) — fixed from 2.5x
paper: Section 4.5 trigger table: GraphPrompt=73 files, AgentNode=596 files
paper: GraphRAG-lite = 'unclassified graph baseline' NOT 'text-based' in Section 5.1
paper: TEMPORAL_HISTORY GraphPrompt CTX=0.600, +20pp over BM25 0.400 (3-decimal in Sec 5.2)
paper: Section 3.5 COIR reference uses 0.380 (3-decimal, consistent with Table 4)
paper: Conclusion IMPLICIT_CONTEXT p-value = '(30 queries; p<0.001)' (NOT McNemar p=0.013 which was overall)

known_facts.fact: Dense embedding (MiniLM) helps NL→Code (COIR: +0.260) but hurts Code→Code (Flask: -0.106)

known_facts: BM25 blend in _implicit_retrieve (adaptive fallback when graph<k) helps Flask IMPL +4.6%p
Path boost in _concept_retrieve helps FastAPI SEMA +5.5%p
FastAPI IMPL near 0 regardless of approach — structural signal absent in 928-file corpus
Bigram BM25 corpus dramatically helps EXPL +9.5%p (Flask) by matching compound symbol patterns; minor IMPL regression from IDF shifts (~3/24 queries)

known_facts.fact: FastAPI IMPLICIT_CONTEXT: ground truth 41-55 files per query → R@5 ceiling ~0.12. Cannot improve with k=5
Temporal noise reduction (downweight path matches when >5 files match per word) helps Flask TEMPORAL +20%p, Requests TEMPORAL +10%p
Reverse import threshold 10→30 + 2-hop reverse had no effect on FastAPI (import graph too sparse for 1120-file repo)
